It's part of a three-component system:\n184\t\n185\t1. **handy-cli** (this project) - CLI wrapper for Claude Code\n186\t2. **handy** - React Native mobile client\n187\t3. **handy-server** - Node.js server with Prisma (hosted at https://api.happy-servers.com/)\n188\t\n189\t## Code Style Preferences\n190\t\n191\t### TypeScript Conventions\n192\t- **Strict typing**: No untyped code (\"I despise untyped code\")\n193\t- **Clean function signatures**: Explicit parameter and return types\n194\t- **As little as possible classes**\n195\t- **Comprehensive JSDoc comments**: Each file includes header comments explaining responsibilities.\n196\t- **Import style**: Uses `@/` alias for src imports, e.g., `import { logger } from '@/ui/logger'`\n197\t- **File extensions**: Uses `.ts` for TypeScript files\n198\t- **Export style**: Named exports preferred, with occasional default exports for main functions\n199\t\n200\t### DO NOT\n201\t\n202\t- Create stupid small functions / getters / setters\n203\t- Excessive use of `if` statements - especially if you can avoid control flow changes with a better design\n204\t- **NEVER import modules mid-code** - ALL imports must be at the top of the file\n205\t\n206\t### Error Handling\n207\t- Graceful error handling with proper error messages\n208\t- Use of `try-catch` blocks with specific error logging\n209\t- Abort controllers for cancellable operations\n210\t- Careful handling of process lifecycle and cleanup\n211\t\n212\t### Testing\n213\t- Unit tests using Vitest\n214\t- No mocking - tests make real API calls\n215\t- Test files colocated with source files (`.test.ts`)\n216\t- Descriptive test names and proper async handling\n217\t\n218\t### Logging\n219\t- All debugging through file logs to avoid disturbing Claude sessions\n220\t- Console output only for user-facing messages\n221\t- Special handling for large JSON objects with truncation\n222\t\n223\t## Architecture & Key Components\n224\t\n225\t### 1. API Module (`/src/api/`)\n226\tHandles server communication and encryption.\n227\t\n228\t- **`api.ts`**: Main API client class for session management\n229\t- **`apiSession.ts`**: WebSocket-based real-time session client with RPC support\n230\t- **`auth.ts`**: Authentication flow using TweetNaCl for cryptographic signatures\n231\t- **`encryption.ts`**: End-to-end encryption utilities using TweetNaCl\n232\t- **`types.ts`**: Zod schemas for type-safe API communication\n233\t\n234\t**Key Features:**\n235\t- End-to-end encryption for all communications\n236\t- Socket.IO for real-time messaging\n237\t- Optimistic concurrency control for state updates\n238\t- RPC handler registration for remote procedure calls\n239\t\n240\t### 2. Claude Integration (`/src/claude/`)\n241\tCore Claude Code integration layer.\n242\t\n243\t- **`loop.ts`**: Main control loop managing interactive/remote modes\n244\t- **`types.ts`**: Claude message type definitions with parsers\n245\t\n246\t- **`claudeSdk.ts`**: Direct SDK integration using `@anthropic-ai/claude-code`\n247\t- **`interactive.ts`**: **LIKELY WILL BE DEPRECATED in favor of running through SDK** PTY-based interactive Claude sessions\n248\t- **`watcher.ts`**: File system watcher for Claude session files (for interactive mode snooping)\n249\t\n250\t- **`mcp/startPermissionServer.ts`**: MCP (Model Context Protocol) permission server\n251\t\n252\t**Key Features:**\n253\t- Dual mode operation: interactive (terminal) and remote (mobile control)\n254\t- Session persistence and resumption\n255\t- Real-time message streaming\n256\t- Permission intercepting via MCP [Permission checking not implemented yet]\n257\t\n258\t### 3. UI Module (`/src/ui/`)\n259\tUser interface components.\n260\t\n261\t- **`logger.ts`**: Centralized logging system with file output\n262\t- **`qrcode.ts`**: QR code generation for mobile authentication\n263\t- **`start.ts`**: Main application startup and orchestration\n264\t\n265\t**Key Features:**\n266\t- Clean console UI with chalk styling\n267\t- QR code display for easy mobile connection\n268\t- Graceful mode switching between interactive and remote\n269\t\n270\t### 4. Core Files\n271\t\n272\t- **`index.ts`**: CLI entry point with argument parsing\n273\t- **`persistence.ts`**: Local storage for settings and keys\n274\t- **`utils/time.ts`**: Exponential backoff utilities\n275\t\n276\t## Data Flow\n277\t\n278\t1. **Authentication**: \n279\t   - Generate/load secret key → Create signature challenge → Get auth token\n280\t\n281\t2. **Session Creation**:\n282\t   - Create encrypted session with server → Establish WebSocket connection\n283\t\n284\t3. **Message Flow**:\n285\t   - Interactive mode: User input → PTY → Claude → File watcher → Server\n286\t   - Remote mode: Mobile app → Server → Claude SDK → Server → Mobile app\n287\t\n288\t4. **Permission Handling**:\n289\t   - Claude requests permission → MCP server intercepts → Sends to mobile → Mobile responds → MCP approves/denies\n290\t\n291\t## Key Design Decisions\n292\t\n293\t1. **File-based logging**: Prevents interference with Claude's terminal UI\n294\t2. **Dual Claude integration**: Process spawning for interactive, SDK for remote\n295\t3. **End-to-end encryption**: All data encrypted before leaving the device\n296\t4. **Session persistence**: Allows resuming sessions across restarts\n297\t5. **Optimistic concurrency**: Handles distributed state updates gracefully\n298\t\n299\t## Security Considerations\n300\t\n301\t- Private keys stored in `~/.handy/access.key` with restricted permissions\n302\t- All communications encrypted using TweetNaCl\n303\t- Challenge-response authentication prevents replay attacks\n304\t- Session isolation through unique session IDs\n305\t\n306\t## Dependencies\n307\t\n308\t- Core: Node.js, TypeScript\n309\t- Claude: `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` SDK\n310\t- Networking: Socket.IO client, Axios\n311\t- Crypto: TweetNaCl\n312\t- Terminal: node-pty, chalk, qrcode-terminal\n313\t- Validation: Zod\n314\t- Testing: Vitest \n315\t\n316\t\n317\t# Production Deployment\n318\t\n319\t## How it Runs\n320\thappy-cli runs as **systemd services** on the host (NOT Docker). Two daemon instances:\n321\t\n322\t| Service | systemd unit | HAPPY_HOME_DIR | PID file |\n323\t|---------|-------------|----------------|----------|\n324\t| Default | `happy-daemon.service` | `/root/.happy/` (default) | `/root/.happy/daemon.pid` |\n325\t| Jade | `happy-daemon-jade.service` | `/root/.happy-jade/` | `/root/.happy-jade/daemon.pid` |\n326\t\n327\t## Binary\n328\t- Installed globally: `/usr/bin/happy` (npm package `happy-coder`)\n329\t- Working directory: `/root`\n330\t\n331\t## Systemd Commands\n332\t```bash\n333\t# Status\n334\tsystemctl status happy-daemon\n335\tsystemctl status happy-daemon-jade\n336\t\n337\t# Restart (use with caution)\n338\tsystemctl restart happy-daemon\n339\t\n340\t# Logs\n341\tjournalctl -u happy-daemon -f\n342\tjournalctl -u happy-daemon-jade -f\n343\t```\n344\t\n345\t## Key Environment (from systemd unit)\n346\t- `HAPPY_SERVER_URL=http://188.245.32.161:3000` (direct IP, bypasses Cloudflare)\n347\t- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=512`\n348\t- `IS_SANDBOX=1`\n349\t- Memory limits: `MemoryMax=1G`, `MemoryHigh=800M`\n350\t\n351\t## Updating the CLI\n352\t```bash\n353\tnpm install -g happy-coder@latest\n354\t# Daemon auto-detects version mismatch via heartbeat and restarts itself\n355\t```\n356\t\n357\t# Running the Daemon (Development)\n358\t\n359\t--- daemon CLAUDE ---\n360\t# Happy CLI Daemon: Control Flow and Lifecycle\n361\t\n362\tThe daemon is a persistent background process that manages Happy sessions, enables remote control from the mobile app, and handles auto-updates when the CLI version changes.\n363\t\n364\t## 1. Daemon Lifecycle\n365\t\n366\t### Starting the Daemon\n367\t\n368\tCommand: `happy daemon start`\n369\t\n370\tControl Flow:\n371\t1. `src/index.ts` receives `daemon start` command\n372\t2. Spawns detached process via `spawnHappyCLI(['daemon', 'start-sync'], { detached: true })`\n373\t3. New process calls `startDaemon()` from `src/daemon/run.ts`\n374\t4. `startDaemon()` performs startup:\n375\t   - Sets up shutdown promise and handlers (SIGINT, SIGTERM, uncaughtException, unhandledRejection)\n376\t   - Version check: `isDaemonRunningSameVersion()` reads daemon.state.json, compares `startedWithCliVersion` with `configuration.currentCliVersion`\n377\t   - If version mismatch: calls `stopDaemon()` to kill old daemon before proceeding\n378\t   - If same version running: exits with \"Daemon already running\"\n379\t   - Lock acquisition: `acquireDaemonLock()` creates exclusive lock file to prevent multiple daemons\n380\t   - Authentication: `authAndSetupMachineIfNeeded()` ensures credentials exist\n381\t   - State persistence: writes PID, version, HTTP port to daemon.state.json\n382\t   - HTTP server: starts on random port for local CLI control (list, stop, spawn)\n383\t   - WebSocket: establishes persistent connection to backend via `ApiMachineClient`\n384\t   - RPC registration: exposes `spawn-happy-session`, `stop-session`, `requestShutdown` handlers\n385\t   - Heartbeat loop: every 60s (or HAPPY_DAEMON_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL) checks for version updates and prunes dead sessions\n386\t5. Awaits shutdown promise which resolves when:\n387\t   - OS signal received (SIGINT/SIGTERM)\n388\t   - HTTP `/stop` endpoint called\n389\t   - RPC `requestShutdown` invoked\n390\t   - Uncaught exception occurs\n391\t6. On shutdown, `cleanupAndShutdown()` performs:\n392\t   - Clears heartbeat interval\n393\t   - Updates daemon state to \"shutting-down\" on backend\n394\t   - Disconnects WebSocket\n395\t   - Stops HTTP server\n396\t   - Deletes daemon.state.json\n397\t   - Releases lock file\n398\t   - Exits process\n399\t\n400\t### Version Mismatch Auto-Update\n401\t\n402\tThe daemon detects when `npm upgrade happy` occurs:\n403\t1. Heartbeat reads package.json from disk\n404\t2. Compares `JSON.parse(package.json).version` with compiled `configuration.currentCliVersion`\n405\t3. If mismatch detected:\n406\t   - Spawns new daemon via `spawnHappyCLI(['daemon', 'start'])`\n407\t   - Hangs and waits to be killed\n408\t4. New daemon starts, sees old daemon.state.json version != its compiled version\n409\t5. New daemon calls `stopDaemon()` which tries HTTP `/stop`, falls back to SIGKILL\n410\t6. New daemon takes over\n411\t\n412\t### Stopping the Daemon\n413\t\n414\tCommand: `happy daemon stop`\n415\t\n416\tControl Flow:\n417\t1. `stopDaemon()` in `controlClient.ts` reads daemon.state.json\n418\t2. Attempts graceful shutdown via HTTP POST to `/stop`\n419\t3. Daemon receives request, calls `cleanupAndShutdown()`:\n420\t   - Updates backend status to \"shutting-down\"\n421\t   - Closes WebSocket connection\n422\t   - Stops HTTP server\n423\t   - Deletes daemon.state.json\n424\t   - Releases lock file\n425\t4. If HTTP fails, falls back to `process.kill(pid, 'SIGKILL')`\n426\t\n427\t## 2. Session Management\n428\t\n429\t### Daemon-Spawned Sessions (Remote)\n430\t\n431\tInitiated by mobile app via backend RPC:\n432\t1. Backend forwards RPC `spawn-happy-session` to daemon via WebSocket\n433\t2. `ApiMachineClient` invokes `spawnSession()` handler\n434\t3. `spawnSession()`:\n435\t   - Creates directory if needed\n436\t   - Spawns detached Happy process with `--happy-starting-mode remote --started-by daemon`\n437\t   - Adds to `pidToTrackedSession` map\n438\t   - Sets up 10-second awaiter for session webhook\n439\t4. New Happy process:\n440\t   - Creates session with backend, receives `happySessionId`\n441\t   - Calls `notifyDaemonSessionStarted()` to POST to daemon's `/session-started`\n442\t5. Daemon updates tracking with `happySessionId`, resolves awaiter\n443\t6. RPC returns session info to mobile app\n444\t\n445\t### Terminal-Spawned Sessions\n446\t\n447\tUser runs `happy` directly:\n448\t1. CLI auto-starts daemon if configured\n449\t2. Happy process calls `notifyDaemonSessionStarted()` \n450\t3. Daemon receives webhook, creates `TrackedSession` with `startedBy: 'happy directly...'`\n451\t4. Session tracked for health monitoring\n452\t\n453\t### Session Termination\n454\t\n455\tVia RPC `stop-session` or health check:\n456\t1. `stopSession()` finds session by `happySessionId`\n457\t2. Sends SIGTERM to process\n458\t3. `on('exit')` handler removes from tracking map\n459\t\n460\t## 3. HTTP Control Server\n461\t\n462\tLocal HTTP server (127.0.0.1 only) provides:\n463\t- `/session-started` - webhook for sessions to report themselves\n464\t- `/list` - returns tracked sessions\n465\t- `/stop-session` - terminates specific session\n466\t- `/spawn-session` - creates new session (used by integration tests)\n467\t- `/stop` - graceful daemon shutdown\n468\t\n469\t## 4. Process Discovery and Cleanup\n470\t\n471\t### Doctor Command\n472\t\n473\t`happy doctor` uses `ps aux | grep` to find all Happy processes:\n474\t- Production: matches `happy.mjs`, `happy` (or legacy `happy-coder`), `dist/index.mjs`\n475\t- Development: matches `tsx.*src/index.ts`\n476\t- Categorizes by command args: daemon, daemon-spawned, user-session, doctor\n477\t\n478\t### Clean Runaway Processes\n479\t\n480\t`happy doctor clean`:\n481\t1. `findRunawayHappyProcesses()` filters for likely orphans\n482\t2. `killRunawayHappyProcesses()`:\n483\t   - Sends SIGTERM\n484\t   - Waits 1 second\n485\t   - Sends SIGKILL if still alive\n486\t\n487\t## 5. State Persistence\n488\t\n489\t### daemon.state.json\n490\t```json\n491\t{\n492\t  \"pid\": 12345,\n493\t  \"httpPort\": 50097,\n494\t  \"startTime\": \"8/24/2025, 6:46:22 PM\",\n495\t  \"startedWithCliVersion\": \"0.9.0-6\",\n496\t  \"lastHeartbeat\": \"8/24/2025, 6:47:22 PM\",\n497\t  \"daemonLogPath\": \"/path/to/daemon.log\"\n498\t}\n499\t```\n500\t\n501\t### Lock File\n502\t- Created with O_EXCL flag for atomic acquisition\n503\t- Contains PID for debugging\n504\t- Prevents multiple daemon instances\n505\t- Cleaned up on graceful shutdown\n506\t\n507\t## 6. WebSocket Communication\n508\t\n509\t`ApiMachineClient` handles bidirectional communication:\n510\t- Daemon to Server: machine-alive, machine-update-metadata, machine-update-state\n511\t- Server to Daemon: rpc-request (spawn-happy-session, stop-session, requestShutdown)\n512\t- All data encrypted with TweetNaCl\n513\t\n514\t## 7. Integration Testing Challenges\n515\t\n516\tVersion mismatch test simulates npm upgrade:\n517\t- Test modifies package.json, rebuilds with new version\n518\t- Daemon's compiled version != package.json on disk\n519\t- Critical timing: heartbeat interval must exceed rebuild time\n520\t- pkgroll doesn't update compiled imports, must use full yarn build\n521\t\n522\t# Improvements\n523\t\n524\tI do not like how\n525\t\n526\t- daemon.state.json file is getting hard removed when daemon exits or is stopped. We should keep it around and have 'state' field and 'stateReason' field that will explain why the daemon is in that state\n527\t- If the file is not found - we assume the daemon was never started or was cleaned out by the user or doctor\n528\t- If the file is found and corrupted - we should try to upgrade it to the latest version? or simply remove it if we have write access\n529\t\n530\t- posts helpers for daemon do not return typed results\n531\t- I don't like that daemonPost returns either response from daemon or { error: ... }. We should have consistent envelope type\n532\t\n533\t- we loose track of children processes when daemon exits / restarts - we should write them to the same state file? At least the pids should be there for doctor & cleanup\n534\t\n535\t- caffeinate process is not tracked in state at all & might become runaway\n536\t- caffeinate is also started by individual sesions - we should not do that for simpler cleanup \n537\t\n538\t- the port is not protected - lets encrypt something with a public portion of the secret key & send it as a signature along the rest of the unencrypted payload to the daemon - will make testing harder :/\n539\t\n540\t--- AC6 test ---\n541\t# Auto-generated by agents/test-writer.md from docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260525-081012.json\n542\t# AC ID: AC6  ac_uid: f3-no-exit-on-spawn-fail  type: hook\n543\t# Dev is free to REPLACE the body of test_AC6() with a real\n544\t# implementation that asserts the GIVEN/WHEN/THEN behaviour.    248	
   249	## Codex adversarial consultation (OPT-IN — only when `--codex` flag set)
   250	
   251	**OPT-IN gating** (2026-05-04 user directive): codex consultation runs ONLY when the orchestrator's dispatch prompt explicitly includes `codex_required: true`; the invoking command is responsible for adding that line when its `--codex` flag applies.
   252	
   253	**When the dispatch does NOT instruct codex** (default — no `--codex` flag): SKIP the Procedure below entirely. Proceed directly to writing your final report. Emit in the JSON report artifact: `codex_consult: { invoked: false, status: "not_requested", findings: [], feedback_summary: null, feedback_incorporated: null }`.
   254	
   255	**When the dispatch DOES instruct codex**: follow the Procedure below. When invoked, codex consultation catches false positives, severity mis-classifications, and scope drift before the report is finalized.
   256	
   257	### Procedure (only when `codex_required: true`)
   258	
   259	1. Complete the full verbosity inspection and draft the findings list in memory (do NOT write the report file yet)
   260	2. Invoke `Skill(skill="codex")` with:
   261	   - Brief inline summary of your draft findings list (pass as text in the prompt, NOT as a file path — do not write a draft file)
   262	   - Explicit instruction (prompt-inspector-role-scoped): "Challenge whether this draft findings list correctly identifies verbosity violations. Flag any false positive (substantive procedural rules flagged as verbose), any missed critical verbosity, any severity mis-classification based on incorrect line-count thresholds. **For every issue you flag, you MUST provide `PROPOSED_FIX: <corrected wording or concrete change>`. A complaint without a PROPOSED_FIX is an observation, not a blocker.** Reply with CODEX_FEEDBACK: <list of issues, each with PROPOSED_FIX or marked OBSERVATION_ONLY>."
   263	3. Parse codex's feedback
   264	4. Incorporate codex feedback proportionally:
   265	   - Findings with a `PROPOSED_FIX`: apply the fix or explain specifically why you disagree — both positions are valid, but silence is not.
   266	   - Findings marked `OBSERVATION_ONLY` (no PROPOSED_FIX): log in `codex_consult.findings[]` with `classification: "observation_only"` and `disposition: "logged"`. Do NOT let bare complaints without a constructive alternative block the cycle.
   267	5. Write the final JSON report artifact with `codex_consult` included — only after step 4
   268	
   269	### Graceful fallback (codex unavailable)
   270	
   271	If `Skill(codex)` returns:
   272	- **Quota error** (e.g. "usage limit", "try again at..."): document `codex_consult: { invoked: true, status: "failed_quota", findings: [], feedback_summary: "<verbatim error message or summary>", feedback_incorporated: "self-review substituted" }` in the JSON report artifact. Proceed with self-review covering 5+ adversarial questions (false positives on procedural rules, line-count threshold accuracy, severity calibration, scope accuracy, wording precision).
   273	- **Hang/timeout** (no response within reasonable time): document `codex_consult: { invoked: true, status: "failed_timeout", findings: [], feedback_summary: "<verbatim error or timeout description>", feedback_incorporated: "self-review substituted" }` in the JSON report artifact. Proceed with self-review as above.
   274	- **Parse error** (codex output unparseable): document `codex_consult: { invoked: true, status: "failed_parse", findings: [], feedback_summary: "<verbatim error or parse failure description>", feedback_incorporated: "self-review substituted" }` in the JSON report artifact. Proceed with self-review as above.
   275	
   276	In all fallback cases, do NOT block the cycle indefinitely. Self-review is acceptable substitute.
   277	
   278	### Output documentation
   279	
   280	Every prompt-inspector JSON output MUST include a top-level `codex_consult` field with this shape:

codex
